```json
{
  "decision": "fail",

  ],
  "percent_complete": 100,

  "sound_source": "pump",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Pump is pumping toward the expansion tank, violating the point-of-no-pressure-change (PONPC) principle.",
    "Incorrect pump location relative to the expansion tank connection causes air binding, noise, and premature pump/boiler failure.",
    "System likely exhibits reduced flow, overheating, and erratic pressure readings."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate the circulator pump to the supply side downstream of the boiler and away from the expansion tank connection.",
    "Ensure the expansion tank is connected to the suction side of the pump at the PONPC.",
    "Purge air from the system and verify flow and pressure after correction."
  ],
  "rationale": "In a hydronic heating system, the expansion tank must be located at the point of no pressure change, and the pump should pump away from it. Pumping toward the expansion tank pressurizes and destabilizes the PONPC, causing air problems, flow issues, noise, and accelerated component degradation. This is a fundamental design/installation error that is non-compliant with hydronic best practices and OEM guidance.",
  "references": [
    "Hydronics references: point-ofno-pressure-change (PONPC) principle",
    "OEM boiler and pump installation manuals"
  ]
}
```
